function.name: database_query
function.description: Execute a read-only SQL query against the database

instruction: How many active users do we have?

arguments.query: SELECT COUNT(*) as active_users FROM users WHERE status = 'active'

function.name: describe_table
function.description: Get the schema of a database table

instruction: Show me the schema of the orders table
result: orders: id (INT PK), user_id (INT FK), total (DECIMAL), status (VARCHAR), created_at (TIMESTAMP), updated_at (TIMESTAMP)

function.name: database_query
function.description: Execute a read-only SQL query against the database

instruction: What were the top 5 products sold last month?

arguments.query: SELECT p.name, SUM(oi.quantity) as total_sold FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE oi.created_at >= '2026-03-01' GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY total_sold DESC LIMIT 5

result: 1. Widget Pro (2,341), 2. Gadget X (1,892), 3. Sensor Kit (1,456), 4. Power Module (1,203), 5. Connector Hub (987)

function.name: database_query
function.description: Execute a read-only SQL query against the database

instruction: Find users who signed up in the last 24 hours

arguments.query: SELECT email, created_at FROM users WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours' ORDER BY created_at DESC

function.name: describe_table
function.description: Get the schema of a database table

instruction: What columns does the events table have?
result: events: id (BIGINT PK), event_type (VARCHAR), user_id (INT), payload (JSONB), timestamp (TIMESTAMPTZ), session_id (UUID)
